questions.question: Who were the 'Samantas'?
questions.answer: Samantas were big landlords or warrior chiefs in different regions of the subcontinent who were acknowledged by existing kings as their subordinates.

questions.question: What did Samantas bring for their kings?
questions.answer: Samantas were expected to bring gifts for their kings or overlords, be present in their courts, and provide them with military support.

questions.question: When did a Samanta become a 'Maha-samanta'?
questions.answer: When Samantas gained power and wealth, they declared themselves to be maha-samanta or maha-mandaleshvara (the great lord of a circle or region).

questions.question: Who was Dantidurga?
questions.answer: Dantidurga was a Rashtrakuta chief who overthrew his Chalukya overlord and performed a ritual called hiranya-garbha to become a Kshatriya.

questions.question: What was the 'Hiranya-garbha' ritual?
questions.answer: Hiranya-garbha literally means the 'golden womb'. It was a ritual performed with the help of Brahmanas to allow a non-Kshatriya to be 'reborn' as a Kshatriya.

questions.question: Mention two dynasties founded by Brahmanas who gave up their traditional professions.
questions.answer: The Kadamba Mayurasharman and the Gurjara-Pratihara Harichandra were Brahmanas who took to arms and established kingdoms in Karnataka and Rajasthan.

questions.question: What high-sounding titles did new kings take?
questions.answer: New kings took titles like Maharaja-adhiraja (great king, overlord of kings) and Tribhuvana-chakravartin (lord of the three worlds).

questions.question: From whom was resources collected in these kingdoms?
questions.answer: Resources were obtained from producers—such as peasants, cattle-keepers, and artisans—who were often persuaded or compelled to surrender part of what they produced.

questions.question: What does the term 'Vetti' mean in the Chola inscriptions?
questions.answer: In Chola inscriptions, 'vetti' refers to a tax taken not in cash but in the form of forced labor.

questions.question: What were the 'Prashastis'?
questions.answer: Prashastis were documents written in praise of rulers. They contained details that might not be literally true but showed how rulers wanted to depict themselves as valiant, victorious warriors.

questions.question: Who was Kalhana?
questions.answer: Kalhana was a 12th-century author who composed a long Sanskrit poem containing the history of the kings who ruled over Kashmir.

questions.question: What made Kalhana different from other writers of Prashastis?
questions.answer: Unlike other writers who only praised kings, Kalhana was often critical about rulers and their policies, using a variety of sources like inscriptions and eyewitness accounts.

questions.question: What was the 'Tripartite Struggle'?
questions.answer: The Tripartite Struggle was a long conflict between the Gurjara-Pratihara, Rashtrakuta, and Pala dynasties for control over the city of Kanauj.

questions.question: Why did kings build large temples?
questions.answer: Kings built large temples to demonstrate their power, wealth, and devotion to gods.

questions.question: Who was Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni?
questions.answer: He was a ruler from Afghanistan who raided the Indian subcontinent almost every year between 1000 and 1025 CE, targeting wealthy temples like Somnath.

questions.question: Who was Al-Biruni?
questions.answer: Al-Biruni was a scholar whom Mahmud of Ghazni asked to write an account of the subcontinent. He wrote the famous Arabic work 'Kitab-al-Hind'.

questions.question: Who were the Chahamanas?
questions.answer: The Chahamanas, later known as the Chauhans, were rulers of the region around Delhi and Ajmer who fought against the Chalukyas and Gahadavalas.

questions.question: Who was the most famous Chahamana ruler?
questions.answer: Prithviraja III (1168-1192) was the most famous Chahamana ruler. He defeated Sultan Muhammad Ghori in 1191 but lost to him the very next year.

questions.question: How did the Cholas rise to power?
questions.answer: Vijayalaya, from the ancient family of the Cholas, captured the Kaveri delta from the Muttarayar in the middle of the ninth century and built the town of Thanjavur.

questions.question: Who is considered the most powerful Chola ruler?
questions.answer: Rajaraja I is considered the most powerful Chola ruler. He became king in 985 CE and expanded Chola control over most of the area.

questions.question: Which two big temples were built by Rajaraja and Rajendra Chola?
questions.answer: The big temples of Thanjavur and Gangaikonda-cholapuram are architectural marvels built by Rajaraja and Rajendra.

questions.question: What were Chola bronze images famous for?
questions.answer: Chola bronze images are considered amongst the finest in the world. Most were of deities, but some were of devotees as well.

questions.question: What is an 'Ur' in Chola administration?
questions.answer: An 'Ur' was a settlement of peasants which became prosperous with the spread of irrigation agriculture.

questions.question: What was the 'Sabha'?
questions.answer: The Sabha was an assembly of prominent Brahmana landholders that looked after the affairs of the brahmadeya (land gifted to Brahmanas).

questions.question: How were members of committees chosen in the Sabha?
questions.answer: Names of eligible members were written on palm leaf tickets and put into an earthenware pot. A young boy was then asked to pick the tickets one by one for each committee.
